compiled_state.title: [reviewer-base.md]: Step 1 'REPO_ROOT=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)' contradicts the 'do NOT re-derive' rule -> DIFF_VALID: no in worktree dispatch
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview
reviewer-base.md contains an internal contradiction: the Step 2 guidance ("Use the REPO_ROOT value provided in your dispatch prompt; do NOT re-derive REPO_ROOT via `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`") is contradicted by the Step 1 code example itself, which sets `REPO_ROOT=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)` before invoking verify-review-diff.sh.

## Failure mode
Reviewer sub-agents dispatched to review a worktree start their Bash CWD in the orchestrator's primary directory (per Claude Code sub-agent semantics). When Step 1's bash command runs `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` there, REPO_ROOT resolves to the orchestrator's repo root, NOT the worktree path the reviewer is supposed to review. verify-review-diff.sh then computes the current diff hash against the orchestrator's tree and returns DIFF_VALID: no, forcing REVIEW_RESULT: error.

## Empirical evidence (2026-04-19 session)
Two reviewer dispatches hit this: (1) initial review of 21d7-b84a fix succeeded only because orchestrator and sub-agent were in the same worktree; (2) review of ba0a-475d fix from agent-a24f3cb6 worktree returned REVIEW_RESULT: error on first dispatch, required re-dispatch with explicit `cd "$REPO_ROOT"` prefix.

## Fix direction
Update reviewer-base.md Step 1 to prefix bash calls with cd: `cd "$REPO_ROOT" && "$REPO_ROOT/.claude/scripts/dso" verify-review-diff.sh "$DIFF_FILE_PATH"` — and document that REPO_ROOT comes from the dispatch prompt, not from `git rev-parse`. After editing, run build-review-agents.sh to regenerate all 10 reviewer agent files.

## Files
- plugins/dso/docs/workflows/prompts/reviewer-base.md (source)
- plugins/dso/agents/code-reviewer-*.md (all regenerated)

comments.body: Triaged (debug-everything Bug-Fix Mode): bug targets an LLM-behavioral skill/agent/prompt file. Per CLAUDE.md rule 22 and behavioral-testing-standard Rule 5, any modification requires dedicated bot-psychologist investigation and explicit user approval. DEFERRED: batch-escalating to end-of-session user review; no code change applied this session.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Investigation (cluster B, sonnet, KERNEL): Taxonomy #16 Instruction Locality + #8 Recency Dominance. ROOT_CAUSE: reviewer-base.md has internal self-contradiction — Step 1 line 59 sets `REPO_ROOT=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)` (concrete bash binding), Step 2 line 70 says "do NOT re-derive REPO_ROOT" (abstract prose). Concrete executable code in Step 1 wins over abstract warning in Step 2. Step 3 repeats the same contradictory derivation at line 244. PROPOSED_FIXES: (a) Step 1: replace `REPO_ROOT=$(git rev-parse ...)` with `cd "$REPO_ROOT"` (REPO_ROOT from dispatch prompt); add inline comment "REPO_ROOT is provided in your dispatch prompt — do NOT re-derive via git rev-parse". (b) Step 3: remove duplicate `REPO_ROOT=$(git rev-parse ...)` line. (c) Run build-review-agents.sh to regenerate all 10 code-reviewer-*.md files. Confidence: high. Minimal-fix applied: true.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: USER_APPROVAL (2026-04-20): User directive — 'Fixes recommended by bot-psychologist are approved' for all Category D intent-confirmed clusters. Cluster B fix approved: reviewer-base.md Step 1 + Step 3 remove redundant REPO_ROOT derivations, use 'cd "$REPO_ROOT"' pattern from dispatch. Build-review-agents.sh regen required to propagate to all 10 code-reviewer-*.md files.
comments.author: Test
